idk if he kept that in this
ye some sort of jump cancel protection
i have nasty stuff in my neck and head
specifically, issues with the cervical plexus and trigeminal neuralgia
cervical plexus = nerve roots from the rami of c1, c2, c3, and c4 vertebrae
i.e., a collection of nerves from your upper spine that gather in your neck
i have issues with the cutaneous nerves, which affect something called my dermatomes
dermatome = region of skin innervated by a nerve
so i get numbness and sometimes pain in various regions of my head and neck and shoulders
those are motor nerves, not sensory nerves
motor nerve = muscle function, sensory nerves = sensation
Back to the PMD grind while I chill here.
i most likely have what's called "neurapraxia" of these sensory nerves, which means low-level damage to the axons or myelin sheath protecting the axons
sorry, i know way too much about the topic lmao
also i shouldn't include the axons in there
i think big words are there to confuse people
the big words are precise terms
I blame English class for encouraging me to use consise words.
doctors are absolutely useless when it comes to neurology
trigeminal neuralgia is easy to describe in simple words: sharp, stabbing pains throughout the face
That sucks I hate that feeling
I mean yeah when my limbs fall asleep
Like when I sleep over my arm and my hand just feels like millions of tiny needles
That's even worse sorry to hear that
not really. there's a surgery if you qualify for it
one problem is that many cases are idiopathic, meaning no cause can be identified
i don't really know. neurology wait times here are literally like 5 years
unless you're dying, you're not being seen for years
look at this shit i got from the neurology clinic https://imgur.com/ejn4XxM
my appointment's on june 22nd, at least
how do they even have patients for the next 5 years booked
follow-ups are almost always shorter wait times
i managed to worm my way into a shorter wait time through sheer persistence and calling myself instead of just waiting for them to review my referral
when i called, the lady was like "it's probably gonna be six years," but i managed to get it to take 3 months, in part because this neurologist sets aside a full day to do the specific testing i need
which my doctor knew, and is why i was referred there in the first place
moral of the story: advocate for yourself
your cat sounds like a nice gentleman/lady
very funny chart for expected vs actual wait times: https://imgur.com/n8Hz8do
yeah fuck you if you're not in urgent need
those are referrals FROM your pcp
and they wonder why people go to emerg
welcome to my life for years, except for in my head and neck lmao
they told me to come back if i have facial paralysis
did they fuck up the IV lmao
with nerves, it can come and go for the rest of your life
